course.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor in TypeScript 7 and Node 24
course.description: Advanced TypeScript backend and web learners ready to integrate authorization, web standards, runtime validation, bounded Fetch and Promise ownership, Cheerio, Playwright, accessibility auditing, secure reporting, testing, operations, and recovery into one cumulative crawler product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative crawler and accessible site auditor from explicit owner authorization through url and robots policy, bounded fetch, cheerio extraction, fair promise scheduling, durable checkpoints, metadata and accessibility audits, playwright rendering, secure reports, operations, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate node 24 and typescript 7 source-to-emission proof; whatwg url and html; rfc 9309; sitemaps; robots-parser conformance adapter; p-limit; cheerio 1.2; playwright 1.61; axe-core 4.12; runtime, security, accessibility, privacy, testing, package, container, and incident evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled compiler, emitted node, loopback http, dns, filesystem, browser, accessibility, load, package, container, restore, rollback, legal-review, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative unfamiliar-site production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves stakeholder outcome, owner authorization, revision and runtime, seed and URL identity, origin and path policy, robots decision, response and byte budgets, parser and extraction provenance, frontier and Promise ownership, privacy and accessibility requirement, failure, cleanup, recovery, and controlled transfer limit before adding one boundary.

Browser TypeScript uses original fixed fixtures and pure bounded models only. It does not import Node APIs, contact sites, resolve DNS, read host files, run Cheerio, Playwright, or axe-core, retain credentials, start timers or processes, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, package, container, or production behavior.

Passing work requires prediction, exact intermediate evidence, runnable accepted and rejected cases, a changed case, a test that catches a deliberate defect, accessible stakeholder output, count reconciliation, and explicit ownership of remaining risk.

credential.title: Build and Ship an Authorized Web Crawler and Accessible Site Auditor in TypeScript 7 and Node 24 Certificate

modules.title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence
modules.description: A request to audit a public website names pages to collect but no owner, user decision, permitted purpose, stop contact, privacy boundary, or proof that findings are useful. Learners cumulatively produce authorized inclusive site-audit charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.
Record who controls each target, permitted identities, paths, methods, times, data uses, and revocation contact before any automated request.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.

modules.title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, ESM, Dependencies, and Emitted Artifacts
modules.description: The tool runs through a development loader from an unknown revision with floating packages, unchecked emitted JavaScript, warm residue, and no clean-install proof. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible source-to-emitted crawler repository through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, ESM semantics, package manager, and supported platforms.
Pin Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, and axe-core 4.12.1 with provenance and license review.
Bind strict source, declarations, maps, generated fixtures, package exports, emitted JavaScript, and startup entry to one revision.

modules.title: Ports, Immutable Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle
modules.description: Modules call global fetch, mutate shared URL and record objects, float Promises, replace AbortSignals, and cannot list remaining handles at shutdown. Learners cumulatively produce explicitly owned crawler component graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, render, audit, storage, and report dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Create readonly branded identities only after unknown runtime values pass current validation and normalization.
Assign each Promise a caller, settlement path, concurrency slot, cancellation rule, error destination, and shutdown consequence.

modules.title: Seed Registry, Origin Scope, Budgets, and Stop Policy
modules.description: A seed silently expands through subdomains, downloads, calendars, search forms, query permutations, and logout URLs without page, byte, time, or incident limits. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default crawl-scope policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.
Define allowed schemes, hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default admission.
Specify methods, path prefixes, query keys and values, fragments, pagination, downloads, forms, and content types as testable rules.

modules.title: WHATWG URL Resolution, Identity, Unicode, and Scope Admission
modules.description: Relative links, base elements, default ports, fragments, percent encodings, Unicode hosts, credentials, and query order create duplicates or cross policy boundaries. Learners cumulatively produce conservative WHATWG URL identity pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Parse URLs with WHATWG URL and inspect scheme, credentials, host, port, path, query, and fragment before trust decisions.
Resolve relative references against the final response URL and the first valid document base while retaining both provenance values.
Remove fragments from fetch identity while preserving document-location fragments when findings need them.

modules.title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter
modules.description: The crawler fetches robots from the wrong origin, trusts a library without conformance fixtures, applies first-match rules, and treats outages as permission. Learners cumulatively produce RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.
Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.
Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.

modules.title: Sitemap XML, Index Traversal, Location Rules, and Admission
modules.description: A sitemap parser accepts unlimited nested indexes, non-UTF-8 surprises, foreign hosts, duplicate URLs, invalid escaping, and freshness hints as commands. Learners cumulatively produce bounded sitemap discovery ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate sitemap and sitemap-index namespaces, UTF-8 XML, escaping, location count, size, and required loc values.
Bound index depth, files, URLs, bytes, redirects, duplicate entries, duration, and cycles before traversal.
Resolve and admit sitemap and page locations through authorization, origin, robots, URL, and SSRF policy.

modules.title: Node Fetch, Redirect Control, Streams, Timeouts, and Bounded Bodies
modules.description: Each request creates unrelated policy, follows redirects automatically, checks only Content-Length, buffers unlimited decoded bodies, and loses abort causes. Learners cumulatively produce bounded Fetch response adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Set honest User-Agent, Accept, conditional validators, safe methods, credentials policy, and manual redirect handling through one adapter.
Classify transport, status, media type, length, encoding, validator, and domain outcome before parsing.
Count compressed, decoded, consumed, and retained bytes during stream processing and cancel over-budget bodies.

modules.title: Redirect Revalidation, DNS, Address Classes, Proxies, and SSRF
modules.description: A URL passes string validation once, then redirects or resolves to loopback, private, link-local, metadata, mixed-address, or rebinding targets through an ambient proxy. Learners cumulatively produce connection-time SSRF defense record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Allowlist schemes and ports, reject embedded credentials, and prohibit non-HTTP destinations before resolution.
Classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Resolve every Location and reapply authorization, scope, credential, method, DNS, loop, and budget rules per hop.

modules.title: Media Types, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity
modules.description: The parser consumes a partial compressed response, guesses platform encoding, ignores conflicting declarations, and hashes normalized text as source bytes. Learners cumulatively produce byte-to-Unicode admission ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations.
Use a documented media-type and conservative sniff policy while rejecting binary, multipart, and ambiguous content.
Reconcile transport, BOM, HTML declarations, decoder support, replacement characters, and confidence with source offsets.

modules.title: WHATWG-Informed HTML Trees, Cheerio, parse5, and Parser Differentials
modules.description: Learners treat source indentation as the DOM, assume Cheerio runs browser JavaScript, ignore malformed-tree repair, and never compare parser options. Learners cumulatively produce Cheerio tree-construction evidence adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish source tokens, parse5-repaired tree, Cheerio selections, browser DOM, accessibility tree, and rendered state.
Choose document, fragment, HTML, or XML loading options deliberately and test wrapper insertion and case behavior.
Test misnesting, omitted elements, tables, templates, foreign content, duplicate attributes, and foster parenting.

modules.title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction
modules.description: A broad selector takes the first node, missing and empty values collapse together, strings are cast into domain types, and markup drift silently corrupts output. Learners cumulatively produce runtime-validated extraction contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Represent absent, empty, malformed, duplicate, conflicting, and admitted values as distinct outcomes.
Keep attributes, text, JSON-LD, configuration, and plugin output unknown until validators produce domain values.

modules.title: Record Schemas, Field Provenance, Confidence, and Data Quality
modules.description: Extracted strings lose page, response, selector, transform, and rejection evidence, making corrections and quality claims impossible. Learners cumulatively produce loss-aware versioned site record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.
Attach source URL, response identity, node or attribute path, raw value digest, transform version, time, and decision to each field.
Use confidence only when tied to observable rule evidence, calibration, thresholds, and review consequences.

modules.title: HTML Link Semantics, Base URLs, Relations, and Discovery Decisions
modules.description: Every href becomes a page request; base, rel, hreflang, media, downloads, forms, non-navigation links, and nofollow policy are ignored. Learners cumulatively produce semantic link-discovery ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Enumerate link-bearing HTML elements and distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, form, media, and embedded destinations.
Apply first-valid-base rules against the final response URL and retain base source and rejected alternatives.
Interpret rel tokens, download, target, media, hreflang, canonical, alternate, and nofollow under an explicit discovery policy.

modules.title: Crawl Graph, Frontier State Machine, Scheduling Identity, and Invariants
modules.description: URLs move through ad hoc arrays, visited is marked after fetch, duplicates race into workers, and retry or cancellation loses causal state. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic frontier transition model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, failed, retryable, terminal, and canceled transitions.
Atomically reserve admitted URL identity when scheduling so concurrent discovery cannot duplicate effects.
Prioritize owner commitments, depth, page classes, freshness, origin fairness, and retries with stable tie rules.

modules.title: Infinite URL Spaces, Calendars, Facets, Sessions, and Budget Defense
modules.description: Calendar next links, faceted queries, session IDs, sort permutations, soft errors, and generated paths consume the crawl while each URL looks valid. Learners cumulatively produce URL-space trap classifier and containment policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Detect calendar, pagination, faceting, sorting, search, session, nonce, path-depth, repeated-segment, and parameter-combination expansion.
Budget distinct keys, values, combinations, orderings, and per-template pages with stakeholder-approved exceptions.
Classify soft 404, login, challenge, empty shell, redirect loop, and repeated-template responses using multiple signals.

modules.title: Origin Fairness, Politeness, Retries, Backoff, and p-limit
modules.description: One global concurrency number bursts a single origin, retries synchronize, Retry-After is ignored, and p-limit is treated as a complete scheduler. Learners cumulatively produce publisher-respecting origin scheduler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Maintain per-origin concurrency, minimum spacing, next-eligible time, request and byte budgets, and owner overrides.
Retry only classified transient and idempotent cases with attempt, elapsed, and owner budgets.
Parse valid Retry-After, combine it with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter, and record the chosen delay.

modules.title: Promise Workers, Backpressure, Abort Propagation, and Clean Drain
modules.description: Promise.all starts the whole frontier, one rejection abandons siblings, streams outlive jobs, and shutdown clears timers without awaiting owned work. Learners cumulatively produce bounded abortable worker pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Admit work through page, origin, byte, memory, browser, file, and report budgets before creating expensive Promises.
Classify per-item failure without losing sibling settlements, aggregate cause, or crawl-level stop conditions.
Propagate capacity from report and storage sinks through parsing, fetch, frontier, and discovery instead of buffering without bound.

modules.title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume
modules.description: The process serializes mutable objects occasionally, writes in place, cannot distinguish complete from partial files, and resumes stale in-flight work as success. Learners cumulatively produce versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.

modules.title: Exact Duplicates, Near Duplicates, Change Detection, and Version History
modules.description: URL equality, source hash, normalized text, SimHash-like similarity, and canonical hints are mixed into one destructive dedup decision. Learners cumulatively produce multi-signal content identity and change ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compute and label wire, decoded, DOM, main-content, record, and rendered digests for distinct questions.
Separate URL aliases, byte-identical responses, equivalent admitted records, and replayed observations without deleting provenance.
Use tokenization, boilerplate policy, thresholds, examples, and false-merge review for similarity decisions.

modules.title: Titles, Canonicals, Robots Directives, Hreflang, and Structured Metadata
modules.description: The audit equates metadata with search-engine truth, trusts canonical and hreflang strings, ignores HTTP directives, and reports isolated tags without graph context. Learners cumulatively produce evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Audit title and description presence, uniqueness, length evidence, visible consistency, and stakeholder intent without ranking promises.
Resolve canonical links and reconcile redirects, sitemap URLs, internal links, content identity, and scope before reporting conflicts.
Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by agent, precedence, response, and scope while keeping crawl and index policy distinct.

modules.title: Static Accessibility Heuristics, Semantics, and Human Review
modules.description: The crawler counts alt attributes and headings, then declares WCAG conformance without rendered states, names, keyboard use, contrast, or human judgment. Learners cumulatively produce WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review.
Audit page language, heading outline evidence, landmark purpose, labels, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers without enforcing cosmetic templates.
Identify native control and relationship evidence while acknowledging accessible names and states can depend on computed browser semantics.

modules.title: axe-core Browser Scans, Interaction States, and Manual Accessibility Evidence
modules.description: One axe scan at page load becomes a conformance certificate; hidden menus, errors, dialogs, narrow layouts, keyboard paths, and false positives are ignored. Learners cumulatively produce stateful automated-plus-manual accessibility review through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Run axe-core with declared version, rules, tags, inclusions, exclusions, page state, and complete result evidence.
Scan initial, navigation, dialog, form error, authenticated, loading, empty, and changed responsive states reached through user actions.
Preserve rule, impact, targets, HTML snippet digest, help, occurrence grouping, false-positive review, owner, and retest evidence.

modules.title: Resource Inventory, Delivery Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Boundaries
modules.description: The audit sums HTML attributes as transfer size, confuses lab and field data, ignores cache and render context, and claims user performance from a crawler timer. Learners cumulatively produce bounded resource and performance evidence report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inventory document, script, style, image, font, media, preload, module, and third-party references with initiator and scope evidence.
Separate encoded transfer, decoded body, in-memory, intrinsic media, and rendered dimensions.
Record cache headers, validators, redirects, protocol, compression, connection context, and cold or warm assumptions.

modules.title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence
modules.description: Every page launches a browser, shares cookies and storage, waits for network idle, captures unstable DOM, and leaves contexts or downloads alive. Learners cumulatively produce isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.
Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.
Wait for application-specific stable evidence under time and request budgets instead of treating network idle as universal readiness.

modules.title: Authenticated Crawls, Sessions, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy
modules.description: Credentials are copied into configs, sessions cross origins and users, logout links execute, sensitive pages enter shared caches, and retained reports leak personal data. Learners cumulatively produce least-authority session and privacy policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require explicit owner and account authorization, purpose, role, environment, page scope, time window, and revocation for authenticated access.
Acquire secrets through approved channels, avoid source and logs, scope them minimally, rotate exposure, and destroy temporary material.
Permit safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms and logout or destructive routes, and test same-site side effects.

modules.title: Untrusted Markup, Formula Injection, Paths, Logs, and Plugin Boundaries
modules.description: Extracted text becomes HTML, CSV formulas execute, URLs create paths, terminal controls poison logs, and third-party plugins receive raw secrets and network authority. Learners cumulatively produce safe untrusted-content processing boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization.
Preserve data while neutralizing spreadsheet formula triggers under an explicit export and consumer contract.
Generate owned filenames, confine paths, reject traversal and unsafe archive entries, and handle collisions and Unicode deliberately.

modules.title: JSON Lines, CSV, HTML Reports, Accessibility, and Correction Workflows
modules.description: Exports omit schema and provenance, spreadsheet formulas remain active, large tables trap keyboard users, charts rely on color, and corrections cannot trace source findings. Learners cumulatively produce versioned accessible site-quality report suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Version JSON Lines records, encoding, ordering, missingness, provenance, error rows, and digest manifest for streaming consumers.
Define headers, delimiters, newline, quoting, Unicode, formula defense, nulls, row limits, and round-trip fixtures with csv-stringify.
Provide landmarks, headings, summaries, sortable-table alternatives, keyboard controls, focus, reflow, status, print, and non-color severity.

modules.title: node:test, Fixtures, Compile-Negative Cases, Faults, and Mutation Evidence
modules.description: Tests mock all networking, reuse friendly HTML, assert snapshots only, skip emitted code, and never prove a deliberate defect is detected. Learners cumulatively produce risk-layered deterministic crawler test system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, boundary, changed, Unicode, duplicate, inaccessible, and privacy-sensitive fixtures with provenance.
Test URL, robots, sitemap, state, scheduling, extraction, dedup, report, and recovery invariants with deterministic clocks and schedules.
Use expected TypeScript errors to prove invalid state, unsafe narrowing, mutable values, and missing exhaustive cases are rejected.

modules.title: Causal Telemetry, Event-Loop Capacity, Scheduling, and Operations
modules.description: Logs lack run and URL identity, metrics have unbounded labels, event-loop delay is ignored, schedules overlap, retention grows forever, and alerts lack owner action. Learners cumulatively produce operable privacy-aware crawler service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate run, policy, URL, attempt, redirect, response, parse, frontier, browser, audit, export, and recovery events without logging content secrets.
Define low-cardinality request, byte, queue, age, error, rejection, browser, report, and budget metrics with units and labels.
Measure event-loop delay, CPU, heap, streams, sockets, browser contexts, queue age, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.

modules.title: Supply Chain, Browser Binaries, Packages, Containers, and Rollout
modules.description: The release trusts lifecycle scripts, downloads an untracked browser, ships dev residue and root permissions, rebuilds after approval, and rolls out without evidence comparison. Learners cumulatively produce source-bound least-authority crawler release through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Review direct purpose, transitive graph, lifecycle scripts, integrity, provenance, licenses, advisories, reachability, and upgrade ownership.
Bind Playwright package, browser revision, download source, digest, platform libraries, sandbox policy, and patch process.
Inspect packed files, exports, declarations, source maps, licenses, fixtures, secrets, generated files, and emitted smoke before publish.

modules.title: Backup, Restore, Incident Response, Release Gates, and Residual Risk
modules.description: A team calls a successful crawl complete without restored checkpoints, corrected exports, browser cleanup, unfamiliar-site evidence, incident drills, or owned residual risks. Learners cumulatively produce production release and recovery defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify policies, authorization records, checkpoints, extracted data, reports, corrections, secrets, and logs by RPO, RTO, retention, and restore order.
Restore into isolation, validate versions and digests, reconcile frontier and records, rotate secrets, and prove safe resume without contacting targets.
Rehearse owner revoke, runaway space, private-address redirect, secret leak, bad selector, accessibility regression, corrupt checkpoint, browser leak, and unsafe export.
